result:
I'd want everyone to have genuine curiosity about perspectives different from their own - not to necessarily agree, but to understand why others see things the way they do.

I think so many problems stem from people talking past each other, assuming the worst intentions, or dismissing different viewpoints without really grasping them first. If we could approach disagreements with genuine curiosity about why reasonable people might think differently, we might find unexpected common ground or at least disagree more productively.

It wouldn't solve everything, but I imagine it would make progress on other challenges much more achievable.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
